notes.anchor: to use audacity instead of wisdom
notes.note: A wry comment on the political climate of the mid-50s BC, when, under the dominance of the triumvirate, boldness counted for more than good sense. The same disenchantment runs through the great apologetic letter to Lentulus (Fam. 1.9).

notes.anchor: you, like Ulysses, will recognize none of your own
notes.note: An allusion to Odysseus's return to Ithaca after twenty years, when his home and people had changed beyond recognition. Cicero jokingly warns Valerius that if he buries himself in rural Apulia he will become a stranger to his Roman friends.
